title: Functional impairment documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports anxiety and worry history, duration, distress, functional impairment, comorbid depression or anxiety, substance use, medical and treatment history, education, and active monitoring.

source.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults with known or suspected generalised anxiety disorder or panic disorder.
source.setting: Primary, community, and specialist mental-health care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult GAD identification, assessment, stepped care, risk, and clinician-led management; not exact for children, every anxiety subtype, or UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.2.11 — identification, assessment, education, and monitoring

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports anxiety and worry history, duration, distress, functional impairment, comorbid depression or anxiety, substance use, medical and treatment history, education, and active monitoring.

assessment.text: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.
assessment.rationale: Supports comprehensive mood, function and contextual assessment for mental-health functional impairment; no decision is generated.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
source.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
source.setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.

assessment.text: Supports anxiety and worry history, duration, distress, functional impairment, comorbid depression or anxiety, substance use, medical and treatment history, education, and active monitoring.
assessment.rationale: Supports anxiety-related distress, function and comorbidity assessment for mental-health functional impairment; no decision is generated.
